request.question: Cult of Personality is a song used by CM Punk as an entrance song for the WWE event held at which New Jersey stadium?

title: WrestleMania 29
Champion (Clement Marfo & The Frontline song)
Luke Gallows
WWE Slam City
King of the Ring (2010)
Elimination Chamber (2013)
Royal Rumble (2013)
Judgment of Princeton
Cult of Personality (song)
WrestleMania XXX
sentences: WrestleMania 29 (billed in print ads as WrestleMania NY/NJ) was the twenty-ninth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE, held on April 7, 2013 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey.
Nine professional wrestling matches were contested at the event, with one being shown on the pre-show.
"Champion" is a single release by English group Clement Marfo & The Frontline.
The song was released in the United Kingdom as a digital download on July 20, 2012.
The song peaked to number 38 on the UK Singles Chart.
The song features on the soundtrack of the video game "F1 2011" and the 2010 film "StreetDance 3D", and was the official theme song of the WWE Royal Rumble pay-per-view in 2013.
The song was also used CM Punk's 434 day reign as WWE Champion as a tribute
Andrew William Hankinson (born December 22, 1983) is an American professional wrestler and promoter, currently signed with WWE on the Raw brand under the ring name Luke Gallows.

During his first stint with the promotion, Hankinson also appeared briefly as an imposter of the wrestler Kane during 2006 and then returned as Festus, a seemingly mentally handicapped character who teamed with Jesse (Ray Gordy), but the team achieved little success.

After disappearing from television, Hankinson later became the enforcer and "disciple" of CM Punk, sporting a cleaner, militant look, with the Festus character's "real identity" revealed as Luke Gallows.
His first run in WWE ended in 2010.

He is perhaps best known for working for New Japan Pro Wrestling under the ring name Doc Gallows, and he also wrestled prominently under the TNA banner, where he was a member of the heel Aces & Eights stable under the ring name D.O.C. (Director of Chaos).

Along with Karl Anderson, Hankinson has won the WWE Raw Tag Team Championship once since returning to WWE, and he also held the IWGP Tag Team Championship three times with Anderson, making them one of only three tag teams (with The Steiner Brothers and The Dudley Boyz) to have held both titles between those promotions.
WWE Slam City was an animated show produced by WWE, which aired on the WWE Network and Wednesday evenings on Nicktoons.
WWE Slam City was based on the Mattel action figure series starring toyetic versions of the WWE roster circa 2013.

The series, filmed in the next generation of stop-motion animation, features a new WWE animated character The Finisher, who fires all of the WWE wrestlers and sends them to Slam City to find day jobs.
The wrestlers are plunged into new career challenges as they pack every street corner with work to do and scores to settle.

With John Cena as an auto mechanic, Rey Mysterio as a traffic guard, Alberto Del Rio as a coffee house barista, Mark Henry as a pizza shop mascot, CM Punk as an ice-cream man, Dolph Ziggler as an industry leader, Randy Orton as a zookeeper, Kane as a chef and Sheamus as a theater usher.
Daniel Bryan, The Miz, Big Show, Brock Lesnar, Santino Marella and Damien Sandow are still jobless but Santino and Sandow are the most desperate to find one.
WWE Legends Stone Cold and The Rock are also featured in Slam City.
The 'Stretch Move' feature of Slam City characters is highlighted in WWE Slam City.
The 2010 King of the Ring was a special edition of "Raw".
that aired on November 29, 2010, at the Wells Fargo Center in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania.
The program featured the nineteenth King of the Ring tournament and first since 2008.
Sheamus defeated John Morrison in the finals of the tournament to become King of the Ring.

Unlike the previous tournament that featured wrestlers from the "Raw," "SmackDown" and "ECW" brands, the 2010 version of the tournament only featured wrestlers from "Raw" and "SmackDown" (following the closure of the ECW brand in February 2010).

The last King of the Ring tournament which only featured wrestlers from both "Raw" and "SmackDown" was held in 2002 (following the WWE Brand Extension the previous month, but before the ECW brand was introduced as a third brand in 2006).

Despite the event being mainly focused on the King of the Ring concept, the main event featured Jerry Lawler (who was celebrating his birthday that day) facing the new WWE Champion The Miz for the WWE Championship in a Tables, Ladders and Chairs match which marked Lawler's first WWE Championship match.
Lawler lost due to distractions from Alex Riley and Michael Cole.
CM Punk was joined in commentary on the show.
Elimination Chamber (2013) (also known as No Escape (2013) in Germany) was a professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.
It took place on February 17, 2013, at the New Orleans Arena in New Orleans, Louisiana.
The pay-per-view was the fourth annual Elimination Chamber event.
This year's event was headlined by The Rock defending the WWE Championship against CM Punk.
Royal Rumble (2013) was the twenty-sixth annual Royal Rumble professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.
It took place on January 27, 2013 at the US Airways Center in Phoenix, Arizona.
It was the first WWE pay-per-view of 2013, and featured John Cena winning the traditional Royal Rumble match.
In the main event, The Rock defeated CM Punk for the WWE Championship, recapturing the title for the first time since 2002, and ending Punk's long reign at 434 days.

The Judgment of Princeton was a wine tasting (or blind tasting) event held on 8 June 2012 during a conference of the American Association of Wine Economists held at Princeton University in Princeton, New Jersey.

The purpose of this event was to compare, by a blind tasting, of several French wines against wines produced in New Jersey in order to gauge the quality and development of the New Jersey wine industry.
Because New Jersey's wine industry is relatively young and small, it has received little attention in the world wine market.

The state's wine production has experienced growth in recent years largely as a result of state legislators offering new opportunities for winery licensing and repealing Prohibition-era laws that have constrained the industry's development in past years.

This event was modeled after a 1976 blind tasting event dubbed the "Judgment of Paris" in which French wines were compared to several wines produced in California when that state's wine industry was similarly young and developing.
The New Jersey wine industry heralded the results and asserted that the rating of New Jersey wines by the blind tasting's judges was a victory for the state's wine industry.
"Cult of Personality" is a song by rock band Living Colour.
It was their second single off their debut album, "Vivid", released on July 14, 1988.
"Cult of Personality" reached No. 13 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and No. 9 on the "Billboard" Album Rock Tracks chart.
It also won the Grammy Award for Best Hard Rock Performance in 1990.
Its music video earned the MTV Video Music Award for Best Group Video and MTV Video Music Award for Best New Artist.
The song was ranked No. 69 on VH1's "100 Greatest Hard Rock Songs".
The solo was ranked No. 87 in "Guitar World"' s "100 Greatest Guitar Solos" list.
It was also selected for inclusion in the musical reference book, "1001 Songs You Must Hear Before You Die: And 10,001 You Must Download".
The sections before the bridge are similar to a hook in Black Sabbath's "Wheels Of Confusion".
In 2007, the song was re-recorded and released for the video game "".
The re-recording later appeared in "Guitar Hero Smash Hits".
It also appeared in the video game "" on the radio station "Radio X".
The song is also the entrance music for former ROH and WWE wrestler turned UFC fighter, CM Punk, and Living Colour performed the song live for his entrance at WrestleMania 29.
The song also appeared on the soundtrack for the 2015 video game "NBA 2K16".
WrestleMania XXX (also written as WrestleMania 30) was the thirtieth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.
It took place on April 6, 2014, at the Mercedes-Benz Superdome in New Orleans, Louisiana.
It was the first WWE event simultaneously broadcast live on pay-per-view television and WWE's new streaming media service, the WWE Network.
Seven professional wrestling matches were scheduled on the event's card and one pre-show match was streamed on the WWE Network.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question` and `passages`, produce the single field `summary`.

Goal: compress the grounded first-hop evidence into a short intermediate state that makes it easy for the next-hop agent to (a) see what facts are present per candidate/entity, (b) see what is ambiguous or missing, and (c) know 1–4 prioritized next-hop retrieval targets or concise queries. This module does not produce the final answer unless the provided passages already unambiguously contain every fact required to resolve the question's relation for every argument.

Procedure:
1) Decide whether the question's relation is semantically complete given the supplied passages. Treat the hop as INCOMPLETE if any of the following hold: a) the relation requires combining or comparing facts across pages (e.g., comparing birth dates, publication/founding years, ordinal ranks, confirming a named director among candidates) and the passages do not supply each required fact unambiguously for all relevant arguments; b) any relation argument is ambiguous or has multiple candidate pages or mentions in the passages; c) an expected candidate page or explicit fact for an argument is missing (e.g., only one side of a comparison is present). Do not trigger INCOMPLETE solely from surface tokens (years, digits) — base the decision on whether the relation's arguments are unambiguously resolved.

2) If the hop is COMPLETE (all required facts to resolve the relation for every argument are present unambiguously in the passages): produce a concise derived result in `summary`. This may be a short phrase or sentence that states the necessary fact(s) needed by the pipeline. Keep it minimal and grounded in the passages; you may include a 1–2 token citation (passage title) if helpful.

3) If the hop is INCOMPLETE or AMBIGUOUS: do NOT assert a final answer or perform the final comparison/selection. Instead emit a short structured report in `summary` containing these labeled elements (prefer bullets or a compact paragraph with explicit labels). Keep all text neutral and non-assertive.

  - evidence: For each named candidate/argument mentioned in the question or passages, list the candidate label and the exact passage title(s) that mention it plus a very short quoted snippet or explicit fact token found there (1–12 words). If a candidate named in the question has no grounded mention in any provided passage, explicitly mark it as NOT present under evidence.

  - ambiguities_or_missing: Explicitly name any ambiguities (e.g., multiple candidates for the same name, multiple pages that could be the intended entity) and any missing expected pages or facts.

  - missing_facts_or_bridge / bridge_entity_or_missing_fact: State exactly which fact(s) or disambiguation is needed to complete the relation. Use a slight conditional labeling here:
      * If the question names an entity that has no grounded mention in the provided passages and the unresolved gap is identity/disambiguation (you cannot map the question token to a single canonical entity needed to ground later facts), label the gap as `bridge_entity_or_missing_fact` and specify that the canonical/biography page for that named entity is required to resolve identity.
      * Otherwise (the missing premise is a concrete attribute/property such as a date, developer, layout engine, filmography item, opening-order token, etc.), use `missing_facts_or_bridge` and describe the exact attribute(s) required (e.g., "birth date for Person A", "developer of Product X"). Do not request the canonical biography page by default in this case; prefer an attribute-targeted retrieval first.

  - retrieval_direction: Provide 1–4 prioritized next-hop targets or concise queries to resolve the missing facts/ambiguities. Order targets by priority and keep them short. Apply this retrieval-priority conditional:
      * If the missing gap is identity/disambiguation (see bridge_entity_or_missing_fact above), put the candidate's canonical/biography page title as retrieval_direction #1 (exact title if known) and include 1–2 biography-style fallback queries (e.g., "<Name> biography", "<Name> official page") as subsequent targets.
      * If the missing gap is a specific attribute/property, put 1–2 concise attribute-focused queries first (e.g., "<Item> developer", "<Item> layout engine", "<Work B> publication year"). Include the canonical/biography page as a secondary target only if it might reasonably contain the attribute and identity resolution could still be needed.

Formatting and style constraints (apply to both COMPLETE and INCOMPLETE cases):
- The output must be a single `summary` field only. Do not emit other fields.
- In INCOMPLETE reports, use neutral, non-assertive phrasing. Do not produce final comparisons, numeric answers, or definitive selections (e.g., avoid "X is the oldest" or "Answer: 3").
- Keep the report compact and actionable (short labeled bullets or one concise paragraph). Avoid long prose.
- It is allowed and encouraged to quote short factual snippets from passages and cite passage titles to show grounding.
- Do not defer or mark INCOMPLETE when the passages already unambiguously provide all facts for the relation and its arguments.

Produce the `summary` content that follows these rules.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the single field `query`.

Goal
- Produce exactly one compact, retrieval-intent query suitable for a second-hop search that will recover the missing bridging evidence needed to answer the question. Do not produce the final answer in `query`.

Semantic decision rule (when to emit a retrieval-intent query)
- If summary_1 does NOT assert a confirmed linking entity or verified bridging fact needed to answer the question, you MUST emit exactly one compact retrieval-intent query. "Does not assert a confirmed bridge" includes short or candidate-only summaries (e.g., a single named entity, a quoted candidate title, or numeric anchors that are not verified) and any case where the visible anchors do not unambiguously identify the needed bridge.
- Narrowed applicability for birth-date disambiguation: If summary_1 lacks a confirmed bridge AND the question explicitly requires a birth-date comparison (e.g., asks who was born earlier or requests birth years) AND the visible inputs (question or summary_1) show ambiguous name candidates AND a suitable disambiguator token (occupation, band, institution, or a parenthetical label) is present or clearly implied in the visible inputs, then emit one compact query that combines: the ambiguous name anchor, the disambiguator copied verbatim from the visible inputs, and a concise birth-term ("born" or "birth year"). In all other cases (including birth-name mappings, page-title targets, non-temporal bridges, or when no usable disambiguator is available), do not append birth/date relation words or invent disambiguators; instead prefer the minimal visible-anchor form (page-title, name-pair, or person+institution) appropriate to the missing bridge.
- If summary_1 already names a confirmed bridge (a clearly identified, verified linking entity or unambiguous metadata that directly connects the question's pieces), you may produce a compact query to fetch supporting evidence, but do NOT produce an answer in the `query` field.
- If summary_1 explicitly states that the bridge cannot be recovered automatically (extreme ambiguity or user-local context requiring clarification), do NOT force a misleading retrieval query. In that case, set `query` to the single short token BRIDGE_UNRECOVERABLE (all caps, no punctuation) so downstream steps can prompt the user for clarification. Do not invent facts.

Query construction rules
- Output exactly one compact line of text as `query`. Keep it short and retrieval-focused (few keywords, no full sentences). Use visible anchors from `question` and `summary_1` and minimal retrieval keywords that express the missing relation (e.g., anchor1 anchor2 which artist recorded both; AlbumTitle album artist genre; 1991–92 NHL season regular season).
- Do not include the final answer, assertions, or user-facing instructions in the `query` field. Avoid polite phrasing, questions to the user, or explanatory text. Prefer noun phrases and relation keywords.
- If the conditional birth-date disambiguation rule above applies, build the query by concatenating: the ambiguous person name, the disambiguator token copied verbatim from the visible inputs (occupation, band, institution, parenthetical), and a single concise birth-term ("born" or "birth year"). Use only the visible disambiguator wording; do not invent or normalize parentheticals.
- Relation-token selection rule: choose a single concise relation token that matches the question's relation (e.g., "born" or "birth year" only for temporal birth queries). Do not append relation tokens when the canonical retrieval strategy is to use a page-title, exact name-pair, or person+institution anchor.
- Do not include the final answer or explanatory text. If your natural impulse would be to write a declarative answer or a clarification sentence in `query`, instead convert that output into a compact retrieval-intent query that targets the missing bridge.
- Preserve semantic fidelity: base the query only on visible anchors and relations in `question` and `summary_1`. Do not invent hidden identifiers or assume unseen facts.

Format contract
- Return exactly one field named `query` whose value is a single short string. If unrecoverable as described above, set `query` to BRIDGE_UNRECOVERABLE.

Examples of disallowed outputs for `query`
- A declarative answer (e.g., "The artist was X.")
- A user-facing clarification sentence (e.g., "Do you mean X or Y?") unless summary_1 explicitly requires clarification — in that case use BRIDGE_UNRECOVERABLE.

Behavioral constraints
- Do not use surface heuristics (token count, punctuation, or quotation marks) as the trigger; apply the semantic rule above.
- Emit only one compact retrieval string when the bridge is missing; do not emit multiple queries or long explanations.

Inputs available: `question`, `summary_1`. Output required: single field `query`.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`. The `summary` must be a concise, post-retrieval integration of the evidence that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Follow these rules when composing `summary`:

- Purpose: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` into a grounded state that retains or resolves the earlier bridge only when the second-hop passages supply relation- or attribute-facts that are necessary to answer or disambiguate the `question` (for example: partnership/membership links, joint-award partners indicated by 'with', or requested biographical qualifiers such as nationality, birthdate, current team/status).

- When to append supporting sentence(s): Append the primary canonical label/token from `context` and at most one short passage-grounded supporting sentence only when a passage contains an explicit, directly stated predicate that links the same entity token from `context` (or a clearly corroborated alias/role/date) to the partner or attribute the question cites. Examples of acceptable explicit predicates include clear statements like 'X with Y', 'X is a member of Y', 'X was born DATE', 'X is a recipient of AWARD', 'X is also called Z', or 'X began tenure in YEAR'. The trigger requires that the passage (a) names the same entity token as in `context` or an alias/role/date that is corroborated by other attributes in `context`, and (b) explicitly asserts the relation or attribute needed by the question. Do not append when the passage only contains the attribute string without an explicit predicate, a nearby mention, an indirect implication, or when the link refers to a different named entity.

- Transitive alias/composition: Do not assert transitive equivalence unless every hop in the chain is explicitly stated as an equivalence in the supplied passages and each hop clearly refers to the same entity. If only a mediated or inferred chain exists (i.e., intermediate links are not explicit equivalences and identity-aligned), suppress the appended support and preserve uncertainty.

- When not to append: Do not append supporting sentences and instead return only the canonical label/token when any of the following apply: the question expects a normalized identifier or single token (examples: binary/multiple-choice label selection between named options, identification-only questions, or explicit token-extraction such as 'middle name'); or the `context` already contains a full, passage-consistent, multi-clause integrated answer. Permit an exception for identification/single-token/binary cases only when the question's semantics truly demand the relation/attribute (for example, determining an era boundary or tenure date) and a passage explicitly provides the necessary predicate linking the context entity to that temporal relation or attribute.

- Identity alignment and verification: Before appending, confirm that the fact in `passages` refers to the same entity referenced in `context` by matching names, corroborating aliases, roles, dates, or other attributes. If identity is ambiguous or unsupported, refrain from appending and preserve uncertainty instead. Avoid naive substring or proximity matches as sufficient evidence.

- Style and content constraints: Keep output concise. If appending, limit `summary` to 1–3 sentences total (the primary concise answer plus at most one short supporting sentence per relevant entity or one joint supporting sentence). Preserve the exact canonical label/token selected from `context`. Use a compact paraphrase grounded in passage language, avoid inventing facts or long verbatim quotations, and avoid misattributing attributes from different entities.

- If there is insufficient information in `context` and `passages` to resolve the needed relation/attribute, summarize the uncertainty succinctly rather than asserting unsupported facts.

Produce only the `summary` field as the integrated output string.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the fields `answer`.

Use only summary_1 and summary_2 as evidence. Treat summary_2 as the later, integrated evidence and prioritize it when reconciling information; use summary_1 only as the earlier, pre-second-hop context. Do not assume the final state is unresolved solely because summary_1 contains bridging or retrieval-oriented language.

Produce exactly one field, answer, whose value is the minimal final answer string that is fully supported by the visible summaries. The answer field must contain only that minimal plain-text string and nothing else (no explanation, no surrounding quotes, no JSON wrappers, no debugging or transport artifacts).

Decide whether the question's intent is binary (a truth-value / yes-no question) or non-binary (asking for a name, title, date, place, numeric count, selection, or other factual value). Make this decision semantically (based on the question's meaning), not by surface heuristics like token counts or punctuation.

When multiple co-referent surface strings for the same entity appear in summary_2, you may prefer a shorter canonical label only if that exact shorter string appears verbatim in summary_2 either as an independent label or as an explicit parenthetical alternate, AND emitting that shorter string alone would not drop administrative or category qualifiers, required punctuation/abbreviations, or other disambiguating tokens that the question requires; otherwise emit the minimal multi-token or formatted string exactly as supported by summary_2. Preserve multi-token formatted place names, parenthetical acronyms or alternates when they are present or when the question's intent relies on them.

- If the question's intent is binary AND both summary_1 and summary_2 unambiguously agree on the same truth-value, emit exactly one lowercase token: either yes or no (and nothing else) as the answer value.

- For all other question intents (non-binary), emit the minimal factual string supported by the summaries exactly as needed: preserve multi-token values, necessary punctuation, full date formats, and place formats (for example, full names, "City, State", or "Month Day, Year"), and do not truncate these to a single token.

If the summaries conflict, are ambiguous, or do not jointly support an unambiguous minimal answer, do not fabricate or over-assert; follow the system's established fallback behavior for ambiguous or unsupported questions.

Always forbid emitting serialized queries, JSON/dict-like structures, internal-debug wrappers, or other transport artifacts as the final answer. The answer must be plain text only.

Make all judgments about minimality and agreement based on the semantic content of summary_1 and summary_2, with priority to summary_2 when they differ.

Produce the answer value exactly as specified above in the answer field.

response.hop1_query: Cult of Personality is a song used by CM Punk as an entrance song for the WWE event held at which New Jersey stadium?
hop1_docs: Cult of Personality (song) | "Cult of Personality" is a song by rock band Living Colour.  It was their second single off their debut album, "Vivid", released on July 14, 1988.  "Cult of Personality" reached No. 13 on the "Billboard" Hot 100 and No. 9 on the "Billboard" Album Rock Tracks chart.  It also won the Grammy Award for Best Hard Rock Performance in 1990.  Its music video earned the MTV Video Music Award for Best Group Video and MTV Video Music Award for Best New Artist.  The song was ranked No. 69 on VH1's "100 Greatest Hard Rock Songs".  The solo was ranked No. 87 in "Guitar World"' s "100 Greatest Guitar Solos" list.  It was also selected for inclusion in the musical reference book, "1001 Songs You Must Hear Before You Die: And 10,001 You Must Download".  The sections before the bridge are similar to a hook in Black Sabbath's "Wheels Of Confusion".  In 2007, the song was re-recorded and released for the video game "".  The re-recording later appeared in "Guitar Hero Smash Hits".  It also appeared in the video game "" on the radio station "Radio X".  The song is also the entrance music for former ROH and WWE wrestler turned UFC fighter, CM Punk, and Living Colour performed the song live for his entrance at WrestleMania 29.  The song also appeared on the soundtrack for the 2015 video game "NBA 2K16".

WWE: Uncaged | WWE: Uncaged is a compilation album of unreleased professional wrestling entrance theme songs which was released by WWE on December 16, 2016 on online music stores.  The album features multiple tracks that were not available to the general public before the release of the album, most notably the first version of Chris Jericho's theme song "Break the Walls Down", which was composed by Jim Johnston and performed by Adam Morenoff used for his WWE debut back in 1999.  The album also features the acoustic versions of Batista's theme song, "I Walk Alone" and Randy Orton's theme song, "Voices" which were used for their WrestleMania 30 video packages, which aired on WWE programming before WrestleMania to get fans hyped and excited for their matches at the event.

Champion (Clement Marfo & The Frontline song) | "Champion" is a single release by English group Clement Marfo & The Frontline.  The song was released in the United Kingdom as a digital download on July 20, 2012.  The song peaked to number 38 on the UK Singles Chart.  The song features on the soundtrack of the video game "F1 2011" and the 2010 film "StreetDance 3D", and was the official theme song of the WWE Royal Rumble pay-per-view in 2013.  The song was also used CM Punk's 434 day reign as WWE Champion as a tribute

WWE No Mercy | No Mercy is an annual professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.  The first No Mercy was held on May 16, 1999 in Manchester, England.  The event was held annually in October from 1999 to 2008, and from 2003 to 2006 it was exclusive to the SmackDown!  brand.  In 2016, the event returned as a "SmackDown" exclusive following the reinstated brand extension and aired on the WWE Network for the first time.  However in 2017, the pay-per-view will be a Raw branded event.  The song "No Mercy" by Jim Johnston was used as a regular theme song for the events in 2002, 2004, 2006, and 2007.  In the 2016 and 2017 events, the theme song was "No Mercy" by KIT.

Line in the Sand (Motörhead song) | Line in the Sand is a 2004 rock song from WWE's soundtrack album, "".  The song used to be the entrance theme of the professional wrestling alliance, Evolution.  The song is performed by Motörhead.

WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour | The WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour was a professional wrestling televised event held by World Wrestling Entertainment and which took place at the Westpac Stadium in Wellington, New Zealand on March 4, 2006.  The event was taped and later aired on WWE 24/7 in the United States.

Stadium anthem | Stadium anthems or sports anthems or arena anthems are a musical genre identifying songs that are played over the public address systems at stadiums and arenas during breaks in the action to rally the fans.  Unlike college fight songs, most stadium anthems were not written primarily for use at sports events, though compilations such as "ESPN Presents Stadium Anthems" and the ""Jock"" series occasionally feature remixed versions of these songs designed to segue together or to accentuate the rhythm or other elements of the songs.  Some football events have their own anthems, but they are not played during breaks, but they are played as entrance music.  The most notable anthems are the FIFA Anthem and the UEFA Champions League Anthem.
hop1_titles: WWE SmackDown Road to WrestleMania 22 Tour
response.summary_1:
- evidence:
  - "Cult of Personality" (passage 1): "the entrance music for ... CM Punk" and "Living Colour performed the song live for his entrance at WrestleMania 29."
  - "CM Punk" (passage 1): referenced as the wrestler using the song.
  - "WrestleMania 29" (passage 1): mentioned as the event where the song was performed.
  - Stadium/venue for WrestleMania 29: NOT present in provided passages.
- ambiguities_or_missing:
  - No passage states which stadium or New Jersey venue hosted WrestleMania 29.
  - Possible minor ambiguity: the song is CM Punk's general entrance music (used at multiple events), but the only grounded event in the passages is WrestleMania 29.
- missing_facts_or_bridge:
  - missing_facts_or_bridge: the venue/stadium name that hosted WrestleMania 29 (the New Jersey stadium hosting that WWE event).
- retrieval_direction (priority):
  1. "WrestleMania 29 venue"
  2. "WrestleMania 29 stadium"
  3. "WrestleMania 29 (WWE event) page" or "WrestleMania 29 Wikipedia"
hop2_docs: WrestleMania XXVI | WrestleMania XXVI was the twenty-sixth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by World Wrestling Entertainment (WWE), which took place on March 28, 2010, at the University of Phoenix Stadium in the Phoenix suburb of Glendale, Arizona.  This was the first WrestleMania since WrestleMania XI with a non-title match as a main event, the first WrestleMania to be held in the state of Arizona and the third to be held in an open-air venue, after WrestleMania IX and WrestleMania XXIV.

WrestleMania X8 | WrestleMania X8 was the eighteenth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) produced by the World Wrestling Federation (WWF).  It took place on March 17, 2002, at the SkyDome in Toronto, Canada, the second WrestleMania at that venue (following WrestleMania VI in 1990).  The event marked the final WrestleMania event under the WWF name.  The record-breaking attendance for the SkyDome of 68,237 grossed approximately $6.1 million CAD ($3.9 million USD).  WrestleMania weekend also included WWF Fan Axxess at the Canadian National Exhibition's Automotive Building.

WrestleMania 2 | WrestleMania 2 (sequentially known as WrestleMania II) was the second annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by the World Wrestling Federation (WWF) (although the first WrestleMania was only on pay-per-view in select areas).  It took place on Monday, April 7, 1986, making it the only WrestleMania that was not held on the customary Sunday.  WrestleMania 2 took place at three venues: the Nassau Veterans Memorial Coliseum in Uniondale, New York; the Rosemont Horizon in Rosemont, Illinois; and the Los Angeles Memorial Sports Arena in Los Angeles, California.

WrestleMania 29 | WrestleMania 29 (billed in print ads as WrestleMania NY/NJ) was the twenty-ninth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE, held on April 7, 2013 at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey.  Nine professional wrestling matches were contested at the event, with one being shown on the pre-show.

WrestleMania 31 | WrestleMania 31 was the thirty-first annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by WWE.  It took place on March 29, 2015, at Levi's Stadium in Santa Clara, California.

WrestleMania XIV | WrestleMania XIV was the fourteenth annual WrestleMania professional wrestling pay-per-view (PPV) event produced by the World Wrestling Federation (WWF), which was presented by M&M's and took place on March 29, 1998, at the FleetCenter in Boston, Massachusetts.

The Streak (wrestling) | The Streak was a series of 21 straight victories for professional wrestler The Undertaker (Mark Calaway) at WWE's premier annual event, WrestleMania.  It began at WrestleMania VII in 1991 when he beat Jimmy Snuka, with the final win coming against CM Punk at WrestleMania 29 in 2013; The Undertaker was absent from WrestleMania X (1994) and WrestleMania 2000, owing to injury.  Overall, he defeated 18 men during The Streak, which included three bouts with Triple H and two each opposite Kane and Shawn Michaels, as well as a handicap match against A-Train and Big Show at WrestleMania XIX.  Although part of professional wrestling storyline, this run of victories was described by sports news publication "The 42" as the "greatest winning streak in sport", while Sky Sports likened it to that of boxer Floyd Mayweather.
response.summary_2: WrestleMania 29 was held at MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey.
response.answer: MetLife Stadium in East Rutherford, New Jersey
